text.title: TTS Basics — Provider Routing Decision Tree
text.summary: Routing tree mapping observable input signals to a conclusion referencing a rule from 01-core-rules.xml.

decision-tree.root-question: Does the payload fit in a single call (len(cleaned_text) <= 4000 chars)?

conclusion.verdict: Reject. Route to tts-implementation LongTextTTS chunking — do not silently truncate.
Force provider in {google, azure}. Never pass SSML to OpenAI TTS or ElevenLabs — both produce broken audio.

branch.when: char_count <= 4000 AND ssml_present == false AND voice_clone_required == true

conclusion.verdict: Escalate to tts-implementation ElevenLabs clone_voice path. tts-basics does not handle cloning. Cache key still includes provider=elevenlabs to keep replay safe.

branch.when: char_count <= 4000 AND ssml_present == false AND voice_clone_required == false AND lang_supported == false

conclusion.verdict: Fall back to ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2 OR Google Neural2 — pick by cost cap. Apply rate-limit delay per rule r6.

branch.when: char_count <= 4000 AND ssml_present == false AND voice_clone_required == false AND lang_supported == true AND final_asset == true

conclusion.verdict: OpenAI TTS with model=tts-1-hd. Higher quality justifies 2x cost on shipped audio.

branch.when: char_count <= 4000 AND ssml_present == false AND voice_clone_required == false AND lang_supported == true AND final_asset == false

conclusion.verdict: OpenAI TTS with model=tts-1. Iterate cheap; promote to tts-1-hd only on final pass.
preconditions not met; route to correct methodology or skip.
